title: Genotyping Atp13a4(KO) mice
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"4hm7j","text":"This protocol outlines the step-by-step process for DNA extraction from mouse biopsies and genotyping of Atp13a4(KO) mice using PCR. DNA is extracted from tail or ear biopsies using a simple alkaline lysis method followed by neutralization. PCR amplification is performed using specific primers for Atp13a4, and products are analyzed on an agarose gel to distinguish between wild-type and knockout genotypes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"cogc4","text":"Atp13a4(KO) mouse strain, C57BL/6NJ-Atp13a4em1(IMPC)Bay/Mmnc (RRID:MMRRC_050715-UNC)       ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":36,"length":19},{"style":"sup","offset":43,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6uv","text":"gDNA isolated from ear/tail biopsies","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn640","text":"Genotyping mix: PCR Bio Rapid (2x)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"file0","text":"PCR tubes","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a2dum","text":"UltraPure‱ Agarose (Invitrogen; cat no 16500-500)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":9,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"2lpe","text":"Midori Green Nucleic Acid Staining Solution (Bulldog-bio; cat no MG04: 10 000X)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6nmhb","text":"GeneRuler 100 bp DNA Ladder (VWR; cat no 733-2602)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"emoji","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5517B326BEBA11EF984D0A58A9FEAC02","name":":tm:"}}}}
steps: Put a small piece of ear in a clean Eppendorf tube.

While gently opening the tube, make sure the sample is still inside and add 90 µl of 50mM NaOH and make sure the sample is inside the liquid. If the sample is not fully immersed spin it down (table top centrifuge).
Sample can be stored at 4°C (short term) or at -20°C (long term).
Carefully transfer the lysate in a clean Eppendorf tube, do not carry over hair/tissue.
Vortex thoroughly, make sure tissue is at the bottom of the Eppendorf (spin down if needed).
Neutralize the extract by adding 10 µL of 1M TRIS-HCl (pH8).
Carry out the PCR process under the UV light cabinet. Prepare a Working Dilution (10 µM) of the PCR primers. %s %s
Prepare a mix for all samples containing the following components: %s %s Prepare positive and negative controls per gene and prepare extra volume to account for pipetting variation.
To each PCR tube, add 23 µL from the reaction mixture and 2 µL gDNA (+/- 100ng) from each sample. Keep tubes On ice .
Prepare a 1.5% agarose gel in TBE buffer containing Midori Green (1:10,000).
creator: Sarah van Veen
